algorithms.name: AES
algorithms.basis: FIPS 197. Grover at most halves the effective key strength; AES-192/256 retain >=96/128-bit post-quantum security. CNSA 2.0 selects AES-256.

algorithms.name: 3DES
algorithms.basis: NIST SP 800-131A deprecates Three-Key TDEA and disallows it for encryption after 2023.

algorithms.name: DES
algorithms.basis: DES has a 56-bit key broken by exhaustive search; disallowed (NIST SP 800-131A).

algorithms.name: RC2
algorithms.basis: RC2 is a legacy cipher with known weaknesses and is not approved by NIST.

algorithms.name: MD5
algorithms.basis: MD5 is collision-broken and disallowed for security use (NIST SP 800-131A; RFC 6151).

recommendation.summary: Replace MD5 with SHA-256 or SHA-384.

algorithms.name: SHA-1
algorithms.basis: SHA-1 collisions are practical (SHAttered, 2017); disallowed by NIST SP 800-131A.

recommendation.summary: Replace SHA-1 with SHA-256 or SHA-384.

algorithms.name: SHA-256
algorithms.basis: FIPS 180-4. Grover reduces preimage resistance to ~128 bits, which remains adequate.

algorithms.name: SHA-384
algorithms.basis: FIPS 180-4. Selected by CNSA 2.0 for quantum-resistant hashing.

algorithms.name: RSA
algorithms.basis: Shor's algorithm breaks RSA on a cryptographically relevant quantum computer. NIST IR 8547 (Initial Public Draft, Nov 2024 - DRAFT) plans deprecation of ~112-bit-equivalent (RSA-2048) by 2030 and disallowance by 2035.

recommendation.summary: Migrate to a hybrid KEM (X25519+ML-KEM) for key establishment or ML-DSA for signatures.

options.basis: FIPS 203 (ML-KEM); CNSA 2.0 transition posture; IETF hybrid drafts.
options.tradeoffs: Safe even if one half is broken; library support still maturing.

options.description: Pure ML-KEM-768 once peers support it.
options.tradeoffs: Simplest end state; no classical fallback during transition.

options.description: If migration must wait, ensure RSA >= 3072 and record in the migration plan.

algorithms.name: ECDSA
algorithms.basis: Shor's algorithm breaks elliptic-curve discrete log. CNSA 2.0 and NIST IR 8547 (DRAFT) require migration of ECC signatures to PQC.

recommendation.summary: Migrate signatures to ML-DSA (or SLH-DSA for long-lived/firmware signing).

options.description: SLH-DSA (FIPS 205) for firmware/code signing and long-lived roots.

algorithms.name: ECDH
algorithms.basis: Shor's algorithm breaks elliptic-curve Diffie-Hellman. Harvest-now-decrypt-later makes recorded key exchanges a present-day confidentiality risk.

recommendation.summary: Use a hybrid KEM (X25519 + ML-KEM-768) for key establishment.

options.description: Pure ML-KEM-768 once peers support it.

algorithms.name: DSA
algorithms.basis: Finite-field DSA is broken by Shor and is also deprecated for new use by NIST (FIPS 186-5 removes DSA generation).

algorithms.name: ML-KEM
algorithms.basis: FIPS 203 (ML-KEM), standardized by NIST in 2024. Post-quantum key encapsulation.

algorithms.name: ML-DSA
algorithms.basis: FIPS 204 (ML-DSA), standardized by NIST in 2024. Post-quantum digital signatures.

algorithms.name: SLH-DSA
algorithms.basis: FIPS 205 (SLH-DSA), standardized by NIST in 2024. Stateless hash-based signatures.
